name: [ES-CMP][V2] Create and Update Task from Work Request

has_supporting_activity.description: True when supporting_activity is non-null and non-empty; used for workflow routing.

output.description: Task creation result; has_supporting_activity drives optional Supporting Activity step.

parameters.name: work_request_object
parameters.description: Object with proceed_status, work_request_id, template_name, workflow_id, workflow_name, status, assignee_name, assignee_id, campaign, supporting_activity, due_date, channel, task_title.

description: Create task from work request; case-insensitive campaign lookup; send_email exactly once per path.
prompt_template:
### Goal
Create a task from the work request using CMP tools. Set task owner from assignee. Call send_email exactly once (success or failure) then return; do not call send_email again or re-enter earlier steps.

### Guardrails
1. Campaign lookup: case-insensitive. Find key K in campaign_mapping_object such that K.trim().toLowerCase() === campaign_name.trim().toLowerCase(); use campaign_id = campaign_mapping_object[K]. If no match or campaign_name null/empty, do NOT call create_task_from_work_request; send failure email to CC list only, return Failed with reason.
2. Call create_task_from_work_request, get_cmp_resource, update_task_substep, send_email each at most once per path. After send_email, return immediately.
3. If task_id is null after create, or owner_email_address is null after get_cmp_resource, take failure path: send_email once (to CC only if owner unknown), return Failed. Do not call further tools with null.
4. Pass supporting_activity to next step using the key "supporting_activity" (same as input). Set has_supporting_activity true only when supporting_activity is non-null and non-empty after trim.

### Input
[[work_request_object]] with: proceed_status, work_request_id, template_name, workflow_id, workflow_name, status, assignee_name, assignee_id, campaign, supporting_activity, due_date, channel, task_title.

### Campaign mapping
{"[ES CMP] Test Campaign": "69228e01dc2727478b5f93ea", "[ES-CMP] Test Opal Usecase - Campaign": "6903266dd19827a999a72490", "Event: Test Campaign": "68f243baac75a43a53dfb70a", "Captives & Fronting (2025)": "6967c4318dbb9dc09d43b966", "Event: Baden Baden (2025)": "6967c3b18dbb9dc09d4342a6", "Event: RVS Monte Carlo (2025)": "692d9822465fe318491a02ef", "Financial & Structured Solutions (2025)": "6967c3ce8dbb9dc09d438c29", "Forward in Life (2025)": "692d9845465fe318491a1a0c", "IP/PULSE & Network (2025)": "692d985c465fe318491a612c", "RDS Platform Overall (2025)": "6967c3ee8dbb9dc09d43a0fd", "RDS Property Exposure (2025)": "6967c4068dbb9dc09d43a95f", "Reinsurance Recalibrated (2025)": "692d9831465fe318491a062a"}

### Process
1. Campaign: campaign_name = trim([[work_request_object]]["campaign"]). If campaign_name null/empty, send_email once (CC only: Neil.Mullarkey@optimizely.com, suriya.disha@optimizely.com, Nicola.Dack@optimizely.com, mehreen.rahman@optimizely.com), subject "Task creation failed in CMP", body "Campaign not in mapping."; return {"execution_status": "Failed", "reason": "Campaign not in campaign_mapping_object"}. Else find K in campaign_mapping_object with K.trim().toLowerCase() === campaign_name.trim().toLowerCase(); if not found, same failure email and return. Else campaign_id = campaign_mapping_object[K].
2. Call create_task_from_work_request(work_request_id, title=task_title, campaign_id, due_at=due_date, owner_id=assignee_id, workflow_id). Let task_id = result. If task_id null, send_email once (CC only), return {"execution_status": "Failed", "reason": "Task creation returned null task_id"}.
3. Call get_cmp_resource(resource_type="task", resource_id=task_id). Extract first_workflow_step_name, cmp_task_url, owner_email_address. If owner_email_address null, send_email once (CC only), return {"execution_status": "Failed", "reason": "owner_email_address null"}.
4. Call update_task_substep(task_id, step_name=first_workflow_step_name, assignee_type="user", assignee_name=[[work_request_object]]["assignee_name"]).
5. Call send_email once: recipient_emails=owner_email_address, cc_emails=[Neil.Mullarkey@optimizely.com, suriya.disha@optimizely.com, Nicola.Dack@optimizely.com, mehreen.rahman@optimizely.com], subject="New Task Created from Work Request in CMP", body with cmp_task_url link. Then do not call send_email again.
6. Return. If [[work_request_object]]["supporting_activity"] is non-null and non-empty (after trim): {"execution_status": "Task created successfully", "task_id", "task_title", "campaign_id", "cmp_task_url", "supporting_activity": [[work_request_object]]["supporting_activity"], "work_request_id", "owner_email_address", "due_date", "has_supporting_activity": true}. Else: {"execution_status": "Task created successfully", "task_id", "campaign_id", "cmp_task_url", "has_supporting_activity": false}.
7. Failure path: If any tool call fails or unexpected value, call send_email exactly once (recipient=owner_email_address if available else CC only), subject "Task creation failed in CMP", body failure message; then return {"execution_status": "Failed", "reason": "Task creation failed during execution."}.
